title: Fix static build cache by using cachix
body:
It looks like we always hit the caching issue in Cirrus CI described within https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8313. A solution around that is to use cachix, which has been pre-populated from my local machine.

To push all (runtime and build) dependencies, we can leverage a pre-populated store by:
```
> nix-store -qR --include-outputs $(nix-instantiate nix/default.nix) | cachix push podman
```
The cache can be re-used by everybody to rapidly build static Podman binaries: https://app.cachix.org/cache/podman

@cevich are we able to expose a secret as environment variable in Cirrus? This way we can automatically update the cache for each run.

> @cevich are we able to expose a secret as environment variable in Cirrus? This way we can automatically update the cache for each run.

Yes this is easily possible, and I'm happy to set this up for you.  Just e-mail or private-message me the variable name and what you want it to contain.

> > @cevich are we able to expose a secret as environment variable in Cirrus? This way we can automatically update the cache for each run.
> 
> Yes this is easily possible, and I'm happy to set this up for you. Just e-mail or private-message me the variable name and what you want it to contain.

Thank you! Sent you a mail a few seconds ago.
@saschagrunert you should have received a reply from me w/ the key.  Please let me know if you didn't get my mail and I can copy-paste my reply here instead.

Works, thanks again @cevich! :pray: 

This is ready for review, PTAL @containers/podman-maintainers
(I restarted the failed `int remote fedora-33 root host` task, no idea why it failed I've never seen that error before...hopefully it's just a flake)
